name: Vía Dalmatia and Rivera de Gata Loop
name_original: Perales del Puerto desde Moraleja – circular por la Rivera de Gata – La antigua Vía Dalmatia
description: Cycle 28.3 miles (45.5 km) on this moderate touring route through Rivera de Gata, exploring the ancient Vía Dalmatia near Moraleja.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Moraleja offer a diverse landscape in the Extremadura region of Spain. The area features varied terrain, including riverine environments along the Rivera de Gata and views of the Sierra de Gata Mountains. Cyclists can find routes with mostly paved surfaces, while some sections may include unpaved paths. The region also includes the Borbollón Reservoir, providing opportunities for scenic rides around water bodies. Best touring cycling routes around Moraleja The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Dalmatia and Rivera de Gata Loop, a 28.3 miles (45 km) trail that takes 3 hours 14 minutes to complete, traversing the Rivera de Gata and ancient Vía Dalmatia. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Dam of the Borbollón Reservoir from Moraleja – circular by the Rivera de Gata, a difficult 43.2 miles (69 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and scenic views around the reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Road to Ronda de la Moheda – Rivera de Gata loop from Moraleja, a 23.7 miles (38 km) trail leading through the scenic Rivera de Gata area, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Moraleja is defined by the Rivera de Gata, the Sierra de Gata Mountains, and the Borbollón Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Moraleja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 120 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Moraleja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Moraleja?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Moraleja, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 3 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 11 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Moraleja?
A: The terrain around Moraleja is diverse, featuring riverine environments along the Rivera de Gata and views of the Sierra de Gata Mountains. Many routes have mostly paved surfaces, making them suitable for various bikes, while some segments may include unpaved paths for those seeking a more rugged experience.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes around Moraleja are circular. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31497803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road to Ronda de la Moheda – Torre de Don Miguel loop from La Moheda de Gata&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route covering 42.1 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling around Moraleja?
A: Cyclists can enjoy scenic views of the Sierra de Gata Mountains and the Rivera de Gata. Routes often pass by the Borbollón Reservoir, offering beautiful waterside vistas. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3333558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borbollón Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6047850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Coria&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into longer tours.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, the region boasts a rich cultural legacy. You might encounter picturesque villages like Hoyos, known for its traditional architecture, or pass through areas with historical structures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5690813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villasbuenas de Gata Village Square&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3333532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Moheda de Gata Town Square and Church&lt;/a&gt; are examples of cultural highlights you might discover.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Moraleja?
A: Moraleja, located in Extremadura, Spain, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be quite hot, while winters are typically mild but can have some rainfall.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the presence of 3 easy routes suggests options suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes often feature mostly paved surfaces and gentler gradients, making them more accessible.

Q: Can you recommend a moderate touring route that explores the Rivera de Gata area?
A: A great moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road to Ronda de la Moheda – La Cochina Bath loop from La Moheda de Gata&lt;/a&gt;. This 23.2 km route takes you through the scenic Rivera de Gata area, offering a pleasant ride with moderate elevation changes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Moraleja?
A: The touring cycling routes around Moraleja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 120 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic beauty and diverse options available.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant elevation changes for a more demanding ride?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111178313/dam-of-the-borbollon-reservoir-from-moraleja-circular-by-the-rivera-de-gata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dam of the Borbollón Reservoir from Moraleja – circular by the Rivera de Gata&lt;/a&gt; provide significant elevation changes. This difficult 69.5 km path offers a strenuous workout with rewarding views around the reservoir.

Q: Are there routes that pass by water bodies like reservoirs or rivers?
A: Absolutely. Many routes traverse the Rivera de Gata, offering lush riverine scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1121874316/via-dalmatia-and-rivera-de-gata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Dalmatia and Rivera de Gata Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, taking you along the Rivera de Gata. Additionally, the Borbollón Reservoir is a prominent feature, with routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111178313/dam-of-the-borbollon-reservoir-from-moraleja-circular-by-the-rivera-de-gata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dam of the Borbollón Reservoir from Moraleja&lt;/a&gt; circling its waters.
tips:
Although the town is small, its urban center revolves around the Plaza de España, where the main institutional buildings, such as the Town Hall, are located.

The Town Hall of La Moheda de Gata is located at Plaza de España, 1 (10849), confirming that this square serves as the center of the town's social and administrative life.
The Parish Church of San Isidro Labrador is the main religious building in La Moheda de Gata. It is dedicated to San Isidro Labrador, the patron saint of farmers, reflecting the town's agricultural history.

Perales del Puerto is a small town on an old mountain road called Vía Dalmacia that connects Las Vegas del Alagón with the Castilian-Leonese Altiplano in Castilla y León. It's a good place to stop for a bite to eat and refill your water bottles. You can rest in the park next to the Perales del Puerto Town Hall. There are few restaurants nearby.

The landscape surrounding the town of Moraleja and the entire Rivera de Gata region is a massive network of irrigation canals, many of which can be cycled through on good gravel roads that service the canals.
Moraleja is a town in the Sierra de Gata region in the Árrago river basin. The natural pool of the river and the shaded park to rest at the end of the walk.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This moderate touring bicycle route is approximately 45.5 km long and generally takes about 3 hours and 14 minutes to complete. This duration assumes a steady cycling pace and good fitness.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While some might describe it as suitable for all skill levels, the route is classified as 'moderate' due to its distance (45.5 km) and elevation gain (around 290 meters). It requires good fitness, so it might be challenging for absolute beginners without prior cycling experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Perales del Puerto from Moraleja cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Moraleja, which is a good place to look for parking. Specific parking details are not provided, but as a popular starting point, you should find suitable options within the town.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily features mostly paved surfaces, making it generally accessible for touring cyclists. This contributes to its moderate difficulty rating, as the surfaces are not typically rugged.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in Extremadura, Spain. Generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, and winter might be cooler and potentially wetter.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting spots. You'll find the La Moheda de Gata Town Square and Church, the historic Stone Mill of Perales del Puerto, and you'll cycle alongside the Rivera de Gata Canal. The route also starts and ends in Moraleja.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route follow parts of the official trail known as the Cañada Real de Gata.

faqs.question: Is the Perales del Puerto from Moraleja cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is mostly paved and passes through natural areas, specific regulations regarding dogs on this particular cycling route are not detailed. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are comfortable with the distance and terrain if you plan to bring them along.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or supplies along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Moraleja, and passes through Perales del Puerto, both of which are settlements where you can expect to find amenities like cafes, shops, or restaurants to refuel or resupply.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Rivera de Gata?
faqs.answer: The Rivera de Gata region is known for its natural beauty. Along this route, you can expect pleasant natural environments, potentially including views of the river, and possibly shaded areas or natural pools, typical of the area.
